title: The NAD+ and Sleep Connection: Why Cellular Energy Affects Rest After 40
tags: NAD+, sleep, circadian rhythm, women over 40, cellular health, insomnia
summary: NAD+ is a key regulator of the circadian clock through SIRT1 proteins, and its decline after 40 directly degrades sleep quality and depth. This article explains the bidirectional relationship between NAD+ and sleep, the science behind circadian disruption in midlife, and how targeted supplementation can help restore both energy and rest.
featured_image_alt: Supplements and wellness products supporting NAD+ and sleep health
